$comment: Canonical, human-readable mirror of backend/app/services/automation_intents.py. This is the source of truth for WHAT each skill does and its safety classification; the backend's Python file is the source of truth for validation logic, and frontend/electron/automation/actions.ts is the source of truth for execution. Render deploys `backend/` as an isolated root directory, so these three can't share a build-time import — see docs/ARCHITECTURE.md ('plugins/ holds...') for why that's a deliberate trade-off. Keep all three in sync when you add a skill.

skills.description: Launch an application already installed on the user's computer.
Open a URL in the user's default web browser.
Save a note into Chu Chu's Notes panel.
skills.executor: backend REST call — POST /api/notes (no local OS action needed)

skills.description: Create a reminder in Chu Chu's Reminders panel.
skills.executor: backend REST call — POST /api/reminders (no local OS action needed)

skills.description: Run a web search and summarize the results.
skills.executor: backend REST call — GET /api/browser/search (no local OS action needed)

skills.description: Capture the user's screen and save it to their Chu Chu workspace folder.
Read basic, non-sensitive system info (OS, battery, memory headroom).
Copy text to the user's clipboard.
Open Chu Chu's local workspace folder (notes, screenshots) in the file explorer.
